fields.summary: Add tags and attributes in resource definition

issuetype.description: An improvement or enhancement to an existing feature or task.
issuetype.name: Improvement

fields.description: We need a more descriptive way to describe resources such as volume, a single value is not enough. Hence propose to add tags and attributes in resource definition, both are optional fields. Tags are the resource labels used to group resource by character; attributes are key-value pairs to carry arbitrary information.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

fields.summary: Support Container Storage Interface (CSI) in YARN

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

issuetype.description: A new feature of the product, which has yet to be developed.
issuetype.name: New Feature

Thanks [~cheersyang] for the patch.

Few comments:
 # In {{ResourcePBImpl}}, you also need to change mergeLocalToBuilder, so that this PBImpl can create ResourceInformationProto by considering the new tags and attributes. Could you please check whether it is needed?
 # In {{convertStringStringMapProtoListToMap}}, any checked is needed to ensure key is not null?
 # In ResourceInformation#equals, could we avoid checking tags and attributes in case if its empty or null?
 # In resource-types-5.xml, could we add some resources with tags as well, and have a test based on same ?
And another major point. This Jira is unrelated to CSI and its an improvement for scheduler. In that case, could we mark this as an independent improvement ticket. Thanks.

Hi [~sunilg]

Thanks for the comments, really good ones.
{quote}In {{ResourcePBImpl}}, you also need to change mergeLocalToBuilder 
{quote}
Yes, that is necessary, otherwise when we convert resource information back to protobuf message, tags and attributes will be missing. I have done that in v3 patch, also I added test case in \{{TestResourcePBImpl}} to cover that, thanks for correcting this.
{quote}any checked is needed to ensure key is not null?
{quote}
Better to have that check, added.
{quote}In ResourceInformation#equals, could we avoid checking tags and attributes in case if its empty or null?
{quote}
I don't think that is necessary, as AbstractSet or AbstractMap already implemented that, I think we are fine.
{quote}In resource-types-5.xml, could we add some resources with tags as well, and have a test based on same
{quote}
Yes, added that, also added a test case in \{{TestResourcePBImpl#testParsingResourceTags}}. 

Please take a look, thanks!
Maybe we can move this out from the umbrella Jira as it is a general purpose one so we can do this in trunk, what do you think?

Thanks [~cheersyang].

Yes, lets move this out of the work.

And latest patch looks fine to me as well. Thank you.  I will commit later today.
Thanks [~sunilg], I moved this out and set YARN-8811 to depend on this one.
Thanks [~cheersyang]. Committed to trunk.

SUCCESS: Integrated in Jenkins build Hadoop-trunk-Commit #15212 (See [https://builds.apache.org/job/Hadoop-trunk-Commit/15212/])
YARN-8836. Add tags and attributes in resource definition. Contributed (sunilg: rev 8e5365e277a184ff65f2f6bca2bf037d1a9f3fd0)
* (edit) hadoop-yarn-project/hadoop-yarn/hadoop-yarn-api/src/main/java/org/apache/hadoop/yarn/util/resource/ResourceUtils.java

[~cheersyang], [~sunilg], 

I remember we discussed before that {{Resource.getResources}} method need to do filtering for ResourceInformation. It seems it is not part of the patch. Is there any follow up JIRAs? 

In addition, this patch doesn't change resourcemanager code, which means no UTs run for resourcemanager. From the scope of the patch it should not likely change the rm logics. But I think if we need to do the follow up Jira like I mentioned above, we definitely needs to run rm-related unit tests.

Hi [~leftnoteasy]

This patch only includes basic changes to the resource information, it gives minimal impact to existing code base. The filtering stuff we discussed earlier will be done in a follow-up Jira, by that time we will add some more UT to cover the testing. Hope that makes sense to you.

Thanks

Thanks [~cheersyang] for the patch.

Few comments:
 # In {{ResourcePBImpl}}, you also need to change mergeLocalToBuilder, so that this PBImpl can create ResourceInformationProto by considering the new tags and attributes. Could you please check whether it is needed?
 # In {{convertStringStringMapProtoListToMap}}, any checked is needed to ensure key is not null?
 # In ResourceInformation#equals, could we avoid checking tags and attributes in case if its empty or null?
 # In resource-types-5.xml, could we add some resources with tags as well, and have a test based on same ?
And another major point. This Jira is unrelated to CSI and its an improvement for scheduler. In that case, could we mark this as an independent improvement ticket. Thanks.

Hi [~sunilg]

Thanks for the comments, really good ones.
{quote}In {{ResourcePBImpl}}, you also need to change mergeLocalToBuilder 
{quote}
Yes, that is necessary, otherwise when we convert resource information back to protobuf message, tags and attributes will be missing. I have done that in v3 patch, also I added test case in \{{TestResourcePBImpl}} to cover that, thanks for correcting this.
{quote}any checked is needed to ensure key is not null?
{quote}
Better to have that check, added.
{quote}In ResourceInformation#equals, could we avoid checking tags and attributes in case if its empty or null?
{quote}
I don't think that is necessary, as AbstractSet or AbstractMap already implemented that, I think we are fine.
{quote}In resource-types-5.xml, could we add some resources with tags as well, and have a test based on same
{quote}
Yes, added that, also added a test case in \{{TestResourcePBImpl#testParsingResourceTags}}. 

Please take a look, thanks!
Maybe we can move this out from the umbrella Jira as it is a general purpose one so we can do this in trunk, what do you think?

[~cheersyang], [~sunilg], 

I remember we discussed before that {{Resource.getResources}} method need to do filtering for ResourceInformation. It seems it is not part of the patch. Is there any follow up JIRAs? 

In addition, this patch doesn't change resourcemanager code, which means no UTs run for resourcemanager. From the scope of the patch it should not likely change the rm logics. But I think if we need to do the follow up Jira like I mentioned above, we definitely needs to run rm-related unit tests.

Hi [~leftnoteasy]

This patch only includes basic changes to the resource information, it gives minimal impact to existing code base. The filtering stuff we discussed earlier will be done in a follow-up Jira, by that time we will add some more UT to cover the testing. Hope that makes sense to you.

Thanks
